meanings.definition: the site of an archeological exploration
examples: they set up camp next to the dig

meanings.definition: an aggressive remark directed at a person like a missile and intended to have a telling effect
examples: his parting shot was `drop dead'
she threw shafts of sarcasm
she takes a dig at me every chance she gets

meanings.definition: a small gouge (as in the cover of a book)
examples: the book was in good condition except for a dig in the back cover
there's an interesting excavation going on near Princeton

meanings.definition: the act of touching someone suddenly with your finger or elbow
examples: she gave me a sharp dig in the ribs

meanings.definition: turn up, loosen, or remove earth
examples: Dig we must
turn over the soil for aeration
dig a hole
dig out a channel
She was digging away at her math homework
Lexicographers drudge all day long

meanings.definition: remove, harvest, or recover by digging
examples: dig salt
dig coal
dig the oars into the water
dig your foot into the floor

meanings.definition: remove the inner part or the core of
examples: the mining company wants to excavate the hillside
he jabbed his finger into her ribs
Do you comprehend the meaning of this letter?
